pattern: Twenty-six letters means a single frequency table settles everything: split the letters that actually appear into an odd-frequency group and an even-frequency group, then the answer is just the biggest odd count minus the smallest even count.
hints: Count how many times each of the 26 letters occurs in s; only letters that actually appear matter.
Split those letters into two groups by the parity of their count: one group with odd frequency, one with even frequency.
The answer is the maximum frequency in the odd group minus the minimum frequency in the even group.

edge_inputs.description: the most frequent letter overall is even, the best odd letter is much rarer
several odd-frequency letters, only the largest one should be picked
several even-frequency letters, only the smallest nonzero one should be picked

stress.description: random lowercase strings guaranteed to contain both an odd- and an even-frequency letter
